_comment: IAM Role Assumption Chain — cross-account access pattern
_purpose: Documents the trust policies for cross-account role assumption. No IAM users with long-lived access keys exist. All access follows this chain: authenticated principal → management account role → workload account role → scoped resource access. Each hop produces temporary credentials that expire in 1 hour.
_why_chains_over_users: Long-lived access keys are the most common source of AWS credential compromise. A leaked key is valid until manually rotated — which can take days to detect. A leaked role session token expires in at most 1 hour automatically.
_escalation_prevention: The permission boundary attached to all workload roles prevents privilege escalation even if an attacker obtains valid credentials. They cannot create new roles, attach policies, or modify their own boundary.

cross_account_trust_policy._applies_to: Roles in workload accounts that can be assumed from the management account

session_policy._purpose: Further scopes permissions for the assumed session below what the role allows

assumption_flow.step_1: Developer authenticates via IAM Identity Center (MFA required)
assumption_flow.step_2: Assumes role in management account (PrincipalOrgID condition verified)
assumption_flow.step_3: Assumes cross-account role in target workload account (MFA age < 1 hour verified)
assumption_flow.step_4: Receives temporary credentials scoped by permission boundary
assumption_flow.step_5: Session expires in 1 hour — no renewal without re-authentication
